title: cytisus pauciflorus
intro: Cytisus pauciflorus, also known as the few-flowered broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a shrub native to the mountains of southern Europe, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and expectorant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems, due to its ability to relax smooth muscle and promote the clearance of mucus. Cytisus pauciflorus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues.
